ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301103 covers green tea beverage broadly; no specific code for packaged less-sugar RTD variant. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LN covers ready-to-drink tea; no dedicated code for less-sugar packaged green tea. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for packaged less-sugar green tea beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard commercially packaged bottle of 500 ml, equivalent to approximately 500 g, is a typical single-serve portion for this ready-to-drink green tea beverage.

nutrient_source.title: Green Tea (Less Sugar, Packaged) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Green Tea (Less Sugar, Packaged), cr_id D09020040. The protein value of 12.8 g/100 g is preserved as reported by HPB but flagged as atypically high for a ready-to-drink green tea beverage; product label verification is strongly recommended. Fat is 0.0 g per HPB source, confirmed zero by formulation for this beverage type.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Sweetened Tea Beverages — Analogous Estimate Based on University of Sydney GI Database Entry for Sweetened Tea
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for packaged less-sugar green tea in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 45 (low category) is estimated by analogy with lightly sweetened tea beverages (GI range 35–55) documented in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 500 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (45 × 15.8) / 100 ≈ 7.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar in a packaged sweetened beverage; sodium is low at 3 mg/100 g but the AHA 2018 guideline recommends limiting sugar-sweetened beverages for blood pressure management.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this packaged less-sugar green tea beverage due to its added sugar content (3.16 g/100 g); the no-sugar packaged variant or unsweetened brewed green tea is preferred for blood pressure control.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened packaged beverage with 3.16 g sugar/100 g and zero dietary fibre; ADA guidelines recommend avoiding sugar-sweetened beverages to prevent postprandial glucose excursions.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid this less-sugar packaged green tea beverage and choose the zero-sugar variant or plain brewed green tea, as even reduced-sugar SSBs can contribute to glycaemic load and are discouraged by ADA 2023 standards of care.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Fat is 0 g and sodium is very low at 3 mg/100 g; green tea catechins (EGCG) have documented cardioprotective antioxidant properties, but added sugar in packaged form attenuates the cardiovascular benefit.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, this beverage is acceptable in moderation given its negligible fat and very low sodium, but the no-sugar packaged variant is preferred to maximise the cardioprotective benefit of green tea polyphenols without the added sugar burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Green tea catechins (EGCG) have documented anti-inflammatory properties; fat is 0 g and no RA hard-gate triggers are present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This beverage is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to the anti-inflammatory polyphenol content of green tea; however, the no-sugar variant is preferred to minimise the pro-inflammatory contribution of added sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Green Tea, Less Sugar (Packaged, Ready-to-Drink) is a commercially produced, ultra-processed ready-to-drink (RTD) green tea beverage sold in sealed bottles, widely available in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It is formulated with water, green tea extract, a reduced quantity of added sugar (approximately 3.16 g per 100 ml), and permitted food conditioners. The beverage has a light, mildly sweet, slightly astringent flavour characteristic of green tea, with a thin, watery texture and pale golden-green colour. It is consumed chilled directly from the bottle and is commonly found in convenience stores, supermarkets, and vending machines. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 100 kcal of energy, 12.8 g protein (atypically high — product label verification recommended), 0 g fat, 3.16 g carbohydrate (all as sugar), 0 g dietary fibre, and 3 mg sodium. The protein value of 12.8 g/100 g is flagged as a potential data anomaly for a standard RTD green tea beverage and should be verified against the product label. Fat is confirmed zero by product formulation. Clinically, this beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) despite its reduced-sugar labelling, and is not recommended for individuals with type 2 diabetes or hypertension. Green tea polyphenols, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), confer antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties relevant to cardiovascular and rheumatoid arthritis management. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low category) by analogy with lightly sweetened tea beverages. This product is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and those following halal and gluten-free diets.
common_user_queries: Is less sugar green tea healthy?
Can diabetics drink packaged green tea?
How many calories in a bottle of less sugar green tea?
Is RTD green tea good for weight loss?
What is the sugar content of packaged green tea?
Is less sugar green tea suitable for hypertension?
Green tea less sugar nutrition facts Singapore

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09020040) and are treated as the canonical source for this packaged product. Confidence is set to medium rather than high because the protein value of 12.8 g/100 g is atypically high for a ready-to-drink green tea beverage and may represent a data entry anomaly; product label verification is required before this value can be accepted at high confidence. GI is an analogous estimate, not a direct measurement.
notes: 1. PROTEIN ANOMALY: The protein value of 12.8 g/100 g as reported by HPB (cr_id D09020040) is preserved verbatim from the source but is flagged as atypically high for a ready-to-drink green tea beverage. Standard RTD green tea beverages typically contain 0–0.5 g protein/100 ml. This value should be verified against the physical product label before clinical or dietary use. 2. ZERO-MACRO JUSTIFICATION: fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero by product formulation; this is a water-based beverage with no added fat ingredients. saturated_fat_g is 0.0 — naturally zero as no fat is present. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — naturally zero. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-based beverage. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB source; RTD green tea beverages do not contain dietary fibre. calcium_mg is 0.0 and iron_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB source. 3. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid breakdown data for this specific packaged less-sugar green tea product are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 4. MULTILINGUAL NAMES: This product is sold commercially in Singapore and is catalogued under the HPB Singapore Foodo database. Mandatory Singaporean multilingual names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) have been added to satisfy G6. Chinese simplified: 低糖绿茶（瓶装即饮）; Chinese traditional: 低糖綠茶（瓶裝即飲）; Malay: Teh Hijau Kurang Gula (Botol, Sedia Minum); Tamil: குறைந்த சர்க்கரை பச்சை தேயிலை (பாட்டில், குடிக்க தயார்); Hindi: कम चीनी हरी चाय (बोतलबंद, पीने के लिए तैयार). 5. DIETARY TAGS REVIEW: The input record included dietary tags 'high_protein' and 'keto' which are inconsistent with the nutritional profile (carbohydrate 3.16 g/100 g is not keto-compatible at typical serving sizes, and the protein value is flagged as anomalous). These tags have been removed pending product label verification. The 'low_sodium' tag is supported (3 mg/100 g). 6. ONTOLOGY MATCH TYPE: foodon_match_type and foodex2_match_type are set to 'closest_parent' as the ontology codes represent the nearest parent category for this specific packaged less-sugar RTD green tea product.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Identified protein anomaly (12.8 g/100 g) — preserved from HPB source with prominent flag in notes and confidence_reason.
Iteration 1: Added zero-macro justification for fat_g, saturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, dietary_fibre_g, calcium_mg, iron_mg in notes per g4 rule.
Iteration 1: Documented all -1 sentinel fields with reason and source consulted per g3 rule.
Iteration 1: Added GI analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database with full documentation.
Iteration 1: Corrected health_context to include type2_diabetes with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' and added cardiovascular_disease condition.
Iteration 1: Removed unsupported dietary tags (high_protein, keto) from classification.
Iteration 1: Verified aliases_by_language english array matches flat aliases array exactly.
Iteration 2: Corrected glycemic_load_per_serving to 7.1 (per 500 g standard bottle serving) and updated notes accordingly.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type to 'approximate' as per validator feedback.

Iteration 3: Added mandatory Singaporean multilingual local names — Chinese simplified (低糖绿茶（瓶装即饮）), Chinese traditional (低糖綠茶（瓶裝即飲）), Malay (Teh Hijau Kurang Gula (Botol, Sedia Minum)), Tamil (குறைந்த சர்க்கரை பச்சை தேயிலை (பாட்டில், குடிக்க தயார்)), Hindi (कम चीनी हरी चाय (बோதலबंद, पीने के लिए तैयार)) to local_names to satisfy G6.
Iteration 3: Added all multilingual local name strings to flat aliases array and aliases_by_language to satisfy Q8 and G6.
Iteration 3: Corrected ontology match_type values from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' to satisfy G5 validator requirement (valid values: closest_parent, exact, none).
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
